pattern_recognition: Stack Problem - This problem fits the stack data structure category because it requires LIFO (Last In First Out) operations. The challenge is to maintain the minimum element efficiently alongside standard stack operations.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Maintain a separate array to track the minimum values each time an element is pushed or popped.
approaches.algorithm: For each push, check if the new element is smaller than the current minimum and update the minimum array. For pop, if the popped element is the current minimum, update the minimum from the array.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a second stack to keep track of the minimum values. This allows constant time retrieval of the minimum element.
approaches.algorithm: For each push, compare the new element with the current minimum and push the smaller one onto the min stack. For pop, if the popped element is the current minimum, pop from the min stack as well.

dry_run: Consider the operations: push(3), push(5), getMin(), push(2), getMin(), pop(), getMin(). Step 1: Push 3 onto the stack. Stack: [3], Min Stack: [3]. Step 2: Push 5. Stack: [3, 5], Min Stack: [3]. Step 3: getMin() returns 3. Step 4: Push 2. Stack: [3, 5, 2], Min Stack: [3, 2]. Step 5: getMin() returns 2. Step 6: Pop (removes 2). Stack: [3, 5], Min Stack: [3]. Step 7: getMin() returns 3.
edge_cases: Empty input should handle gracefully without errors. A single element should return that element as both top and min. Duplicates should not affect the min tracking. Large inputs should maintain performance. Overflow should be considered if using fixed-size data types.
common_pitfalls: 1. Not updating the min stack correctly when popping the minimum element. Wrong: Keep the same min without checking. Correct: Pop from min stack when the min is popped. 2. Failing to handle empty stack scenarios. Wrong: Attempting to pop or getMin from an empty stack. Correct: Check if the stack is empty first. 3. Incorrectly maintaining the minimum value when duplicates are present. Wrong: Only tracking unique minimums. Correct: Track all occurrences of the minimum.
